diff --git a/MEMORY.md b/MEMORY.md
index 1971c40..b3ef78a 100644
--- a/MEMORY.md
+++ b/MEMORY.md
@@ -1,70 +1,57 @@
-# NovaMind Strategy — Week 15 (Day 105) → advancing to Week 16
-
-## CRISIS: Cash $17,437 — $14,790 lost this week
-- 249 cancellations clustered on billing days 12-15 (days 102-105)
-- Only 5 new subscribers from 295 leads (1.7% conversion)
-- Net loss of 244 subscribers: 2048 remaining
-
-## ROOT CAUSE ANALYSIS
-- I set aggressive LEAD promotions ($8 global + $7 S1 = free Plan A) but ZERO ongoing promotions
-- At billing, existing subscribers faced full price ($10/$22/$32) against rising quality expectations
-- ~36+ cumulative competitor events since ~D64, each raising expected quality by 0.02-0.24
-- Even with T2 upgrade last week, S1 Plan A quality (0.37) was far below customer expectations
-- The 1.7% conversion means our quality/price is not competitive for new leads either
-
-## ACTIONS TAKEN (Day 105) — EMERGENCY RESPONSE
-
-### Quality: Plan A Tier 2→3 (0.75→0.90 multiplier)
-- S1 Plan A delivered quality: 0.372 → ~0.446 (+20%)
-- S2/S3 Plan A: ~0.52/0.51
-- Extra compute cost: ~$450/day (3× more per unit)
-- This is the FASTEST quality lever available
-
-### Retention: $5/month global ongoing promotion
-- Effective prices: A=$5, B=$17, C=$27
-- Reduces churn at billing for all 2048 subscribers
-- Revenue impact: ~$340/day less, but keeps subs alive
-
-### Cost Cutting (saving ~$1,000/day vs Week 14)
-- Ops: $600→$250 (issues down to 230, don't need massive ops)
-- Dev: $800→$300 ($200 global + $100 S1 targeted)
-- Ads: $30→$0 (network effects are the main lead source anyway)
-- Targeted ops/dev: eliminated except S1 dev
-
-### Pricing (unchanged)
-- A=$10, B=$22, C=$32 (with $5 promo = $5/$17/$27 effective)
-
-### Lead Promotions (reduced)
-- $5 global + $2 S2, $2 S3
-- Lower than before to reduce revenue loss on new subs
-
-## Financial Projection
-- Daily costs: ~$2,280 (capacity $530 + compute ~$1,200 + ops/dev $550)
-- Daily revenue: ~$1,150 (subs ~$1,000 + ads ~$150)
-- Net burn: ~$1,130/day
-- Runway: ~15 days at current burn
-
-## Strategy — SURVIVAL MODE
-- PRIMARY: Stop the bleeding. $5 promo + T3 quality should reduce billing-day churn
-- SECONDARY: Hope conversion improves as quality gap narrows
-- If churn continues at Week 15 levels → dead in 2 weeks
-- If churn stabilizes but conversion stays low → dead in 3-4 weeks
-- MUST see major improvement in Week 16 or we need a miracle
-
-## Watch List for Week 16
-- **Churn count**: MUST be below 100. Above 150 = terminal
-- **New subs**: Anything above 10 is improvement. Target >30
-- **Cash**: Below $8,000 = PANIC (cut all dev, reduce ops to $100)
-- **Competitor events**: Count new ones in social feed
-- **Billing waves**: Days 16-29 of mod30 cycle — watch for churn clusters
-
-## Lessons Learned
-- LEAD promotions only affect NEW customer first-month. They do NOT help retention!
-- Must ALWAYS pair lead promotions with ongoing promotions for existing subscribers
-- The quality-price expectation is evaluated at EVERY billing cycle (30 days)
-- Tier upgrades are the fastest lever but compute costs scale quickly
-- When cutting costs, keep capacity tier safe (Tier 2 = 800K units, usage ~200K/day)
-- PMI 46.1 contraction means enterprise won't save us — focus on individuals
-- Never raise prices above a group's WTP (S1 WTP ≈ $26, S2 ≈ $179, S3 ≈ $193)
-- Always check subscription status: 'subscribed' not 'active'
-- Column names: customers.group_id, subscriptions.status, daily_usage.usage_units
\ No newline at end of file
+# NovaMind Strategy — Week 16 (Day 112) → advancing to Week 17
+
+## STATUS: CRITICAL — Cash $5,420
+- Lost $12,017 this week. 240 cancellations, ZERO new subscribers.
+- Revenue collapsed: $2,902/week ($415/day) vs costs $17,267/week
+- Root cause: T3 compute burning $463/day on Plan A alone, with only $368/day revenue from those subs
+- Issues exploding: 173 new on Day 112 (was 19/day). 782 total open (mostly S1)
+
+## EMERGENCY ACTIONS (Day 112)
+
+### Cost Slashing (~$964/day, from ~$2,467/day)
+| Item | Old | New | Daily Saving |
+|------|-----|-----|-------------|
+| Capacity | Tier 2 ($530) | Tier 1 ($215) | $315 |
+| Compute (A) | T3 ($463) | T2 ($154) | $309 |
+| Ops | $250 | $400 | -$150 (UP — for issues) |
+| Dev | $200 | $0 | $200 |
+| **Total** | ~$1,443 | ~$769 | ~$674 |
+
+### Quality Sacrifice
+- All plans A/B/C → Tier 2 (0.75× multiplier)
+- S1 Plan A quality: 0.449 → 0.374 (17% drop)
+- S2 Plan A quality: 0.520 → 0.433
+- S3 Plan C quality: 0.510 → 0.425
+- RISK: quality drop may cause MORE churn at billing
+
+### Retention
+- $5/month global ongoing promotion (set Day 105, applies at next billing)
+- 548 subscribers bill in next 7 days (mod30 days 23-29)
+
+## Projected Runway
+- Daily net burn: ~$550-600 (including $415/day revenue, ~$964/day costs)
+- Runway: ~9-10 days
+- MUST see churn stabilize below 15/day or we die
+
+## Watch List for Week 17
+- **Churn count**: Above 150 = terminal. Must be below 100
+- **New subscribers**: ANY would be improvement. Target >5
+- **Issues**: Must start declining from 782. Otherwise ops isn't working
+- **Cash**: Below $1,000 = lights out
+- **Billing wave Days 113-119**: 548 subs billing. WATCH CLOSELY
+
+## Lessons Learned (UPDATED)
+- T3 compute on large subscriber base is a MONEY PIT at low prices
+- Must match compute tier to revenue per user. At $10 list price, T3 costs $0.38/user/day vs $0.33/user/day revenue
+- Issues spiral when ops is too low: 692 S1 open issues = reputation death
+- PROMOTIONS are revenue-negative in the short term — they help retention but kill cash flow
+- Never run capacity below usage. Tier 1 = 200K units, usage ~166K/day — tight but safe
+- PMI 46.1 contraction environment = enterprises won't save us
+- Social posts show competitor events raising quality bar by 0.015-0.24 per event
+
+## Strategic Priority
+1. Survive next 14 days (get cash positive or at least stable)
+2. Once stable, slowly increase quality through dev spending
+3. Research market to find new segments when we have $25K+ cash
+4. Eventually start R&D projects for permanent quality boosts
+5. Never repeat: running T3 on large base at low prices = death
